identity.name: Kabir Rao
identity.headline: Creative Director | Brand, Campaigns & Design Systems

summary.short: Creative director who rebrands tired labels and builds the studios that keep them fresh — 12 years across brand, motion and packaging.
summary.long: Creative director working across brand identity, campaigns and design systems. Started as a packaging designer, ran an in-house team at a consumer brand, then built an independent studio that now works with food, fashion and telecom clients. Cares most about the unglamorous half of design — the system, the guidelines, the handoff — because that is what keeps a brand looking like itself a year later.

current_roles.title: Founder & Creative Director
highlights: Rebranded a 20-year-old FMCG label and lifted its shelf recall — sales rose 18% in the two quarters after relaunch.
Built and run a nine-person studio covering brand, motion and packaging for retainer clients in food and fashion.
Directed a national campaign for a telecom launch that reached 40 million people and won a Kyoorius bronze.
Set the design system three product teams now share, so a new landing page ships in a day instead of a week.
Mentored four junior designers; two of them now lead their own pods.

past_roles.title: Senior Brand Designer
highlights: Owned packaging design across a 30-SKU range and cut new-SKU turnaround from six weeks to two.
Shaped the visual language for a summer campaign that ran across 12 cities.

past_roles.title: Art Director
highlights: Art-directed print and film for two FMCG accounts, one of which grew share three points during the work.

projects.name: Handloom Co. identity
projects.description: Full rebrand for a weavers' collective — logo, packaging and a storefront system now used across 40 outlets.

projects.name: Metro transit signage
projects.description: Wayfinding and pictogram set for a metro line, tested with 200 commuters before rollout.

achievements: Rebranded three national labels, each of which grew sales in the year after relaunch.
Grew a solo practice into a nine-person studio profitable in its second year.
Won a Kyoorius bronze and two regional advertising awards for campaign work.
Speak twice a year at design schools on building systems, not just logos.
